hadiths.chapter: 86. CHAPTER ON HORSES, KEEPING THEM, AND THE
hadiths.ar:
2464   -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم مَّاعِي لُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّلَّى إ َّن ِى  ق ُّن َّي ْسٍ ق َّن َّاذَّي َّا إ َّلأَّ هَّلاَّ إ َّلأَّ هَّلُّم َّ ق َّمَّا ن َّق ِى َّ ق َّزَّس  إ ِلأ َّ إ َّعْطَّى ن ِق ِ ي َّاذِةِ وَّ إ َّمْكَّن َّ مِن ْ ن َّاصِي َّي ِهِ
hadiths.en:
And I heard him (Imam {a.s}) say:  
"Whoever leaves their house, or any other house, early in the morning and encounters 
a chestnut horse (ashqar) with white markings (awdah), their day will be blessed. If the 
horse has a prominent flowing white blaze ( ghurra saila ), it signifies prosperity, and 
they will experience joy throughout that day. Moreover, Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and 
Glorious, will fulfill their needs."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 633 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab 
al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 191 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 878 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 477 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, 
p. 170 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 86, H. 2463
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON HORSES, KEEPING THEM, AND THE

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٦، ح ٢٤٦٣

hadiths.chapter: 86. CHAPTER ON HORSES, KEEPING THEM, AND THE
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Horses were once wild in the lands of the Arabs. Ibrahim {a.s} and Isma‘il {a.s} ascended 
Mount Abi Qubays and called out: 'Come! Come forward!'  
No horse remained except that it came forward, offering its reins and submitting its 
forelock."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 86, H. 2464

hadiths.chapter: 87. CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
hadiths.ar:
2466  - وَّ سَّا َّلَّ رَّج ُّل  إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : مَّي َّى إ َّص ْزِب ُّ ذَّإن  َّي ِى  ي َّحْي ِى  ق َّال ك َّ كَّمَّس ْي ِهَّا إ ِلَّى 
مِذ ْوَّذِهَّا
hadiths.en:
Isma‘il ibn Abi Ziyad narrated with his chain of transmission that the 
Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings of Allah {SWT} be upon him and his family) 
said:  
"A mount has certain rights upon its owner:   
- Begin by feeding it when you dismount.  
- Offer it water when you pass by a source.   
- Do not strike its face, for it glorifies its Lord {AZJ} with praise.  
- Do not remain seated on its back except in the way of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and 
Glorious.  
- Do not burden it beyond its capacity.   
Do not compel it to walk more than it can endure."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Khisal, Part 1, p. 330 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 
262 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 829 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 478 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 3, p. 348 • 
Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 201 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 87, H. 2465
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ٢٤٦٥

hadiths.chapter: 87. CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
hadiths.en:
A man asked Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "When is it permissible for me to strike the 
animal I am riding?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "When it does not walk under you as it would walk toward its feeding 
trough."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 6, p. 538 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 
263 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 830 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 487 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 213 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 87, H. 2466
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ٢٤٦٦

hadiths.chapter: 87. CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
hadiths.ar:
2468  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ عَّسْث ِ ي َّق ُّولُّ ي َّعَّسَّ  
إ َّعْصَّان َّا لِلز َّب
hadiths.en:
It is narrated that Imam {a.s} said:  
"Strike them for stumbling, but do not strike them for shying away, for they see what 
you do not see."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 263 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 
831 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 488 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 87, H. 2467
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ٢٤٦٧

hadiths.chapter: 87. CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
hadiths.ar:
2469  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلِى    عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  وَّ ج َّل َّ لَّعَّن َّ لأَّعِن َّهَّا 
وَّ ق ِى  ج َّن َّزٍ إ ج َّز
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings of Allah {SWT} be upon him 
and his family) said:  
"If a mount stumbles under its rider and the rider says to it, 'May you perish!' it 
responds, 'Perished is the one who disobeys our Lord {AZJ} the most.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 6, p. 538 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 6, p. 164 
• Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 263 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 832 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 486 • Wasa’il Al-
Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 487 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 208 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 87, H. 2468

hadiths.chapter: 87. CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
hadiths.en:
Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"Regarding animals: Do not strike their faces and do not curse them, for Allah {SWT}, the 
Mighty and Glorious, curses the one who curses them."   
In another narration, Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"Do not insult their faces."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 87, H. 2469

hadiths.chapter: 87. CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
hadiths.ar: 2471  - وَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ هَّا مَّح َّالِس
hadiths.en:
The Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah {SWT} be upon him and his family) 
said:  
"When animals are cursed, the curse clings to them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 483 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 
61, p. 212 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 87. CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings of Allah {SWT} be upon him 
and his family) said:  
"Do not sit sideways on animals, and do not make their backs your seats."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 6, p. 539 • Al-Adab ad-Diniyah, Part 1, p. 
139 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 263 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 831 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 481 • Bihar 
Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 214 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 87. CHAPTER ON THE RIGHTS OF THE ANIMAL UPON ITS
hadiths.en:
Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}  said:  
"Everything has a sanctity, and the sanctity of animals lies in their faces."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 632 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 6, p. 539 • 
Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 263 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 830 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 482 • Wasa’il Al-
Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 484 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 204

hadiths.chapter: 88. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS NOT BEEN MADE OBSCURE
hadiths.ar:
2474  -  وَّ إ َّم َّا إَّلْح َّن َّزُّ إَّل َّذ ِى  رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال إَّلْمَّوْب ِ مَّا ي َّعْزِق ُّون َّ مَّا 
إ َّكَّلْي ُّمْ مِن ْهَّا سَّمِي ن اً ق َّظ  
ق لي س ن ح لاف  هذ إ إلح ن ز لأ ن ها ي عزف  إلموب  لكن ها لأ ي عزف  من ه ما ي عزق ون .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Abu Hamzah, from Imam Ali ibn al-Husayn {a.s}, that 
he used to say:  
"Animals are not ignorant of four things: their knowledge of their Lord {AZJ}, the Blessed 
and Exalted; their knowledge of death; their distinction between male and female; and 
their knowledge of fertile pasture."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 6, p. 539 • Al-Khisal, Part 1, p. 260 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 875 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 3, p. 401 • Ithbat al-Hudat, Part 1, p. 71 • Bihar Al-
Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 50 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 88, H. 2473
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS NOT BEEN MADE OBSCURE
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS NOT BEEN MADE OBSCURE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٨، ح ٢٤٧٣

hadiths.chapter: 88. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS NOT BEEN MADE OBSCURE
hadiths.en:
As for the narration from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} in which he 
said:  
"If animals knew about death what you know, you would never eat a fat one among 
them."  
[AL SADUQ] 
This does not contradict the previous narration, as animals do recognize death, but 
they do not comprehend it to the extent that humans do.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 876 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 51

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 88, H. 2474

hadiths.chapter: 89. CHAPTER ON THE REWARD FOR SPENDING ON
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings of Allah {SWT} be upon him 
and his family) said regarding the verse of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Glorious:  
"Those who spend their wealth in the night and the day, secretly and openly, they shall 
have their reward with their Lord {AZJ}, and they shall not fear nor grieve." (Surah Al -
Baqarah 2:274)  
Prophet {saws} said: "It was revealed concerning spending on horses."   
[AL SADUQ] 
The compiler of this book - may Allah {SWT} be pleased with him - comments: This verse 
has also been narrated as being revealed concerning Amir al-Mu'minin {a.s}. The reason 
for its revelation was that he had four dirhams: he gave one as charity at night, one 
during the day, one secretly, and one openly. Hence, this verse was revealed 
concerning him.  
It is understood that when a verse is revealed regarding a specific case, it applies to 
all similar situations. Thus, while its specific interpretation is in regard to Amir al -
Mu'minin {a.s}, it also applies to spending on horses and similar acts of charity.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 827 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 
470 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 176 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 291 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 
2, p. 451

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 89, H. 2475
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REWARD FOR SPENDING ON
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REWARD FOR SPENDING ON

hadiths.chapter: 90. CHAPTER ON THE REASON FOR THE TWO PATCHES
hadiths.en:
Hammad ibn Uthman narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
I said to Imam {a.s}, "May I be your ransom! We see markings on the undersides of the 
forelegs of animals, like two patches or brands. What is that?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "That is the place where their nostrils were positioned while they 
were in their mother's womb."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 878

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 90, H. 2476

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him) that he said:  
I heard the Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace of Allah {SWT} be upon him and his family) say:  
"The animal prays: 'O Allah {SWT}, grant me an owner of sincerity who feeds me, gives 
me drink, and does not burden me with more than I can bear.'"   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 262 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 91, H. 2477
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩١، ح ٢٤٧٧

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
hadiths.ar:
2479 - وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْهُّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال  إَّلْحَّوَّإن ِح ُّ وَّ رِر ْق ُّهَّا عَّلَّى  
إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"No one purchases an animal except that it says: 'O Allah {SWT}, make him merciful to 
me.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 262 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 
833 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 479 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 91, H. 2478
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩١، ح ٢٤٧٨

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
hadiths.ar:
2480  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلس َّكُّون ِى  ُّ ن ِا ِسْن َّاذِةِ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ك َّ وَّ ي َّعَّالَّى ي ُّحِث  ُّ إَّلزِّق ْق َّ  
وَّ ي ُّعِي ن ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ رَّكِن ْي ُّمُّ إَّلذ َّوَّإب  َّ إَّلْعِح َّاف َّ ق َّا َّن ْز ِلُّوهَّا مَّن َّار ِلَّهَّا ق َّ ا ِن ْ كَّان َّث ِ إَّلْأ َّرْض ُّ مُّح ْذِن َّه ً ق َّان ْح ُّوإ عَّلَّي ْهَّا وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّث ْ  
مُّح ْصِن َّه ً ق َّا َّن ْز ِلُّوهَّا مَّن َّار ِلَّهَّا
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said that he heard from Imam 
Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}:  
"Acquire a mount, for it is an adornment, needs are fulfilled through it, and its 
sustenance is upon Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Glorious."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 626 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 6, 
p. 164 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 262 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 463 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 
465 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 15, p. 139 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 172 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 91, H. 2479
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩١، ح ٢٤٧٩

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
hadiths.en:
Al-Sakuni narrated with his chain of transmission that the Messenger of Allah 
{SWT} (peace and blessings of Allah {SWT} be upon him and his family) said:  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and Exalted, loves gentleness and facilitates it. When 
you ride lean animals, grant them proper rest. If the land is barren, travel quickly on 
them. If the land is fertile, let them rest in appropriate places."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 361 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 120 • 
Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 262 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 394 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 451 • Bihar Al-
Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 213 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 72, p. 62 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 279 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 91, H. 2480
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩١، ح ٢٤٨٠

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
hadiths.ar:
2482  - وَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم رْض ٍ مُّح ْذِن َّه ٍ 
ق َّعَّح ِّلْ ن ِالس َّي ْزِ
hadiths.en:
Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"Whoever among you travels with an animal should begin, when stopping, by feeding 
and giving water to it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 361 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, 
p. 262 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 395 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 479 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 203 • Bihar 
Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 213 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 91, H. 2481
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩١، ح ٢٤٨١

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON GOOD CARE FOR ANIMALS
hadiths.en:
Abu Ja‘far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"If you travel through fertile land, proceed gently, and if you travel through barren land, 
hasten your journey."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 361 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, 
p. 262 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 395 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 451 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 61, p. 213 • Bihar 
Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 279

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 91, H. 2482
